decisions.relationship_evidence: Re-analyzes 1,327 E. coli genomes with typing and diversity analyses to assess overlap and possible pet-to-human transmission.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic comparison of resistant E. coli from human and companion-animal infections.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses phylogenetic, population-structure, and comparative-genomic analyses to characterize S. aureus lineages in atopic dermatitis.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic epidemiology study of S. aureus from patients in China and global collections.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses phylogenetic clustering, tMRCA, and reproductive-number estimates to reconstruct Omicron sublineage dynamics in Italy.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete SARS-CoV-2 genomic epidemiology application using 8,970 samples.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Describes incorporation of WGS with traditional epidemiology for pathogen-source and transmission-pathway analysis in Welsh healthcare practice.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete account of genomic epidemiology use for COVID-19 and C. difficile management in Wales.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses WGS, phylogenomics, cgMLST, and epidemiological data to identify possible nosocomial Morganella transmission clusters.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete hospital-isolate genomic investigation of possible transmission.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops a novel One Health data-integration framework incorporating pathogen genomic data for real-time surveillance and joint analysis.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicitly develops an operational framework for integrated surveillance and applied genomic epidemiology.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Combines strain-resolved metagenomics and isolate sequencing to identify clonal C. auris spread and bacterial strain sharing in nursing homes.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic reconstruction of pathogen sharing within nursing homes and across a metropolitan region.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Integrates whole-genome phylogenetics with epidemiologic links to distinguish multiple introductions from within-unit C. auris spread.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic epidemiology application to a burn-unit outbreak.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Integrates phylogenomic, temporal, and epidemiological analyses to identify genomic clusters and enteric-fever transmission events.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete reconstruction of Salmonella transmission dynamics in Shenzhen.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses viral genotyping and network analysis to identify hepatitis A genomic clusters with distinct temporal and spatial distributions.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic epidemiology analysis of Florida hepatitis A cases.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses whole genomes, identity-by-descent connectivity, and geographic data to examine P. knowlesi population structure and local transmission patterns.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete parasite genomic epidemiology application in Sabah, Malaysia.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies birth-death-sampling phylodynamic models in three SARS-CoV-2 case studies addressing superspreading, nosocomial transmission, and population structure.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Provides a comprehensive workflow, worked applications, assumption checks, and validation guidance for genomic epidemiology researchers.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Analyzes global and regional mSTM ST34 genomes to characterize population dissemination, resistance, virulence, and multispecies transmission potential.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete large-scale genomic epidemiology study of a zoonotic Salmonella lineage.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Evaluates SNP transmission cutoffs against transmission events inferred by a phylodynamic model in 2,008 tuberculosis genomes.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit performance assessment of genomic-distance thresholds used to infer probable transmission.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses WGS and maximum-likelihood phylogenetics to characterize K. pneumoniae complex population diversity, resistance, and epidemiology.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic epidemiology application to clinical isolates from a Chinese hospital.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses WGS and comparative genomics across 48 isolates to characterize K. pasteurii diversity, clades, and global dissemination.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Combines structured literature and genome searches with phylogenomics to examine M. orygis diversity and cross-host transmission pathways.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Combines whole-genome lineage analysis with a multistrain transmission model to explain contrasting regional lineage dynamics.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete clinical and genomic epidemiology application to invasive S. pyogenes in Australia.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses Bayesian inference, HIV-TRACE, MCODE, and demographic data to infer HIV transmission clusters, origins, hotspots, and spread.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Analyzes 1,506 A. baumannii genomes to characterize population dynamics, clone expansion, and transmission patterns.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses MLST and phylogenetic analysis to characterize resistant P. aeruginosa lineages and assess their potential clonal spread.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses WGS, genetic-context analysis, and conjugation experiments to characterize E. coli lineages and horizontal resistance dissemination.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses WGS, cgMLST, Hamming distances, and epidemiological factors to identify and characterize persistent Salmonella clusters.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Performs genome-wide phylogenetic analysis to identify T. indotineae groups, introductions, and regional clonal transmission clusters.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete fungal genomic epidemiology application spanning China and global isolates.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Provides PopPIPE, a reproducible pipeline that clusters bacterial populations and detects plausible transmission events from genomes.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses whole-genome sequencing and phylodynamics to infer H5N1 origins, incursions, migration routes, and probable transmission networks.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses whole-genome phylogenetics and spatiotemporal analysis to identify ASFV subclusters and multiple introductions into South Korea.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses whole genomes and phylodynamics to reconstruct swine-influenza evolution, spatiotemporal spread, and interprovincial movement.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses core-genome MLST and phylodynamic analyses to characterize MRSA dissemination and identify highly transmissible clones.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops NLP classifiers for extracting patient metadata from sequencing articles; it supports genomic epidemiology but does not itself perform or implement transmission reconstruction.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses WGS, phylogenetics, and mutation-rate analysis to characterize a novel bloodstream-infection cluster and its regional epidemiology.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Discusses the potential to estimate pathogen burden from genomic diversity but reports no developed, evaluated, or applied method.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses metagenomic sequencing to detect respiratory viruses and generate genomes, but does not perform genomic epidemiological or transmission analysis.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Provides sequence-generation input rather than an application or implementation of the exact concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Jointly applies WGS, epidemiological analysis, and phylodynamics to reconstruct MRSA movement between community and hospital settings.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic transmission investigation across healthcare-community interfaces.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Directly demonstrates that widespread near-identical A. fumigatus clones and sparse exposure data severely limit precise source and transmission tracing.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses 582 genomes to resolve C. ulcerans population structure, toxin transfer, and emerging sublineage dissemination.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses phylogenetics, genetic-distance networks, and birth-death phylodynamics to reconstruct a recent HIV transmission outbreak.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete molecular transmission analysis among people who inject drugs.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses WGS, SNP distances, and phylogenetic relationships to infer bidirectional hospital-environment A. baumannii transmission.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete local genomic epidemiology study in Kano, Nigeria.

decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete hospital-system genomic epidemiology study.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies phylogenetic and phylodynamic approaches to reconstruct FY.4 emergence, local spread, and international export.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete SARS-CoV-2 transmission-dynamics study centered on Kenya.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Conducts genomic epidemiology analyses of approximately 200 genomes to characterize hospital clone spread and resistance-gene transfer.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses WGS and epidemiological links to resolve clonal APEC lineages and vertical transmission across poultry generations and farms.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic epidemiology investigation of two colibacillosis outbreaks.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops a multiscale pandemic framework coupling an agent-based transmission model with a within-host phylodynamic model and validates it on SARS-CoV-2.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development and validation of a new transmission-and-evolution modeling framework.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Analyzes nearly 30,000 SARS-CoV-2 genomes to characterize regional lineage diversity, temporal communities, and epidemic waves.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses whole-genome phylogenetics and phylodynamics to investigate epidemiologic links and microevolution during a hospital S. aureus outbreak.

decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete clinical and genomic epidemiology study of a hospital outbreak.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses maximum-likelihood and Bayesian phylodynamics to reconstruct Salmonella Panama population structure, evolution, and geotemporal dissemination.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses genome sequencing to reconstruct a cholera clone's microevolution, cross-regional transmission, and local outbreaks.

decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete national genomic epidemiology application in Bolivia.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses phylogenomic and phylodynamic trees to characterize hMPV introductions, lineage diversity, evolution, and global population recovery.
